questions.q: Harihara and Bukka are famous as the founders of which empire?
questions.explanation: The brothers Harihara and Bukka founded the Vijayanagara Empire in 1336 CE.

questions.q: In which year did Harihara and Bukka found Vijayanagara?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara was founded by the two brothers in 1336 CE.

questions.q: What was the relationship between Harihara and Bukka?
options: They were cousins married to sisters
questions.explanation: Harihara and Bukka were brothers who jointly founded the empire.

questions.q: Which dynasty did Harihara and Bukka establish?
questions.explanation: The brothers founded the Sangama dynasty, the first of Vijayanagara's four ruling lines.

questions.q: On the bank of which river did the brothers establish their new capital?
questions.explanation: They founded their capital on the southern bank of the Tungabhadra river.

questions.q: Which brother became the first king of Vijayanagara?
options: Both ruled jointly with equal title
questions.explanation: Harihara I (Hakka) became the first crowned king of Vijayanagara, with Bukka as his close partner and successor.

questions.q: The capital city founded by Harihara and Bukka grew into which renowned city?
questions.explanation: Their capital grew into Hampi, one of the largest and richest cities of its age.

questions.q: The empire founded by the brothers became a great stronghold of which culture?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara became a leading stronghold of Hindu culture and temple-building in southern India.

questions.q: In which century did Harihara and Bukka found their empire?
questions.explanation: The empire was founded in 1336, in the 14th century CE.

questions.q: The brothers established their kingdom on which side of the Tungabhadra river?
questions.explanation: They founded the capital on the southern bank of the Tungabhadra.

questions.q: After Harihara I, which of the founding brothers succeeded to the Vijayanagara throne?
questions.explanation: Bukka Raya I succeeded his brother Harihara I and consolidated the young empire.

questions.q: The Sangama dynasty was named after whom?
options: Sangama, the father of the founding brothers
questions.explanation: The dynasty took its name from Sangama, the father of Harihara, Bukka, and their brothers.

questions.q: Which sage is traditionally said to have inspired Harihara and Bukka to found Vijayanagara?
questions.explanation: Tradition credits the sage Vidyaranya with inspiring and blessing the brothers' founding of the empire.

questions.q: The early capital of Harihara and Bukka was named in honour of which figure, according to one tradition?
questions.explanation: One tradition holds the city was first called Vidyanagara after the sage Vidyaranya before becoming Vijayanagara.

questions.q: Harihara and Bukka had originally served as officers under which northern power before founding their own kingdom?
questions.explanation: According to several accounts the brothers had served the Delhi Sultanate before establishing the independent kingdom of Vijayanagara.

questions.q: Which northern Muslim kingdom became the chief rival of Vijayanagara soon after its founding?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani Sultanate, founded in 1347, became Vijayanagara's principal rival across the Deccan.

questions.q: Harihara and Bukka are believed to have originally come from which region or background before founding Vijayanagara?
options: The Deccan, in service of regional kingdoms such as the Kakatiyas/Hoysalas
questions.explanation: The brothers are believed to have served Deccan kingdoms like the Kakatiyas or Hoysalas before forging their own state.

questions.q: The founding of Vijayanagara is often seen by historians as a response to the expansion of which power into the south?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara is often interpreted as a consolidation of southern Hindu power in the face of the Delhi Sultanate's southward expansion.

questions.q: During Bukka I's reign, Vijayanagara fought prolonged wars against the Bahmanis over which contested region?
options: The Raichur doab and the Krishna-Tungabhadra frontier
questions.explanation: Bukka I waged repeated wars with the Bahmanis over the fertile lands between the Krishna and Tungabhadra rivers.

questions.q: Bukka I is noted for an act of religious tolerance involving a dispute between which two communities?
questions.explanation: Bukka I is famously recorded as mediating and protecting both Jains and Vaishnavas, declaring them under his equal protection.

questions.q: How many sons of Sangama, including Harihara and Bukka, are traditionally counted as the founding brothers?
questions.explanation: Sangama is traditionally said to have had five sons, of whom Harihara and Bukka were the most prominent founders.

questions.q: The dynasty founded by Harihara and Bukka was eventually succeeded by which line?
questions.explanation: The Sangama dynasty was succeeded by the Saluva dynasty when Saluva Narasimha seized the throne in the late 15th century.

questions.q: According to a widely repeated tradition, before founding Vijayanagara the brothers had been captured and what?
options: Converted to Islam at Delhi, then later returned to Hinduism
questions.explanation: A common tradition holds the brothers were captured by the Delhi Sultanate and converted to Islam, later reconverting to Hinduism under Vidyaranya's guidance, though historians debate this account.

questions.q: Harihara I established the empire's authority partly by absorbing the territory of which declining southern dynasty?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara expanded into the lands of the collapsing Hoysala kingdom in the southern Deccan.

questions.q: Bukka I sent a famous diplomatic mission to which foreign court, recorded in Chinese sources?
questions.explanation: Bukka I is recorded as sending an embassy to the Ming emperor of China in the 14th century.

questions.q: Harihara and Bukka issued their inscriptions and grants invoking which protective deity of the empire?
questions.explanation: The Sangama rulers governed in the name of the god Virupaksha, a form of Shiva, the tutelary deity of the capital.

questions.q: Who succeeded Bukka I as Vijayanagara's ruler, continuing the Sangama line at the empire's early peak?
questions.explanation: Bukka I was succeeded by his son Harihara II, who further expanded the empire toward the coasts.

questions.q: Under Harihara II, Vijayanagara extended its control to reach which two seas, becoming a coast-to-coast power?
options: The Caspian Sea and the Black Sea
The Red Sea and the Mediterranean
The Arabian Sea and the Bay of Bengal
The Andaman Sea and the Java Sea
questions.explanation: Harihara II expanded the realm so that Vijayanagara controlled territory from the Arabian Sea to the Bay of Bengal.

questions.q: The historical reliability of the conversion-and-reconversion story of Harihara and Bukka is best described as:
options: A fact proved by Greek coins
Entirely a 20th-century invention with no early source
A traditional account debated and questioned by modern historians
questions.explanation: The conversion narrative comes from later traditions and is debated; many historians treat it cautiously as legend mixed with history.

questions.q: The brothers' choice of capital site was strategically strong because the location offered what natural defence?
options: A vast desert on all sides
The Tungabhadra river and surrounding rocky granite hills
questions.explanation: The capital was protected by the Tungabhadra river to the north and a rugged landscape of granite boulders and hills, making it easy to defend.
